entry.title: Checklist to Re-Establish SAP Fieldglass Integrations in Sandbox Environments
entry.content:
In the previous SAP Fieldglass Sandbox Environment Refresh: 5 Tips for Success blog, we shared recommendations for preparing your sandbox for a smooth and successful refresh. Now, we’re shifting the focus to a key post-refresh activity – re-establishing integrations in your sandbox environment.

Whether you are performing a system refresh, working through a new implementation, or completing routine maintenance, the steps below will help ensure your sandbox connects properly to your other test systems and supports end-to-end testing.

Pre-Refresh Preparation

Before initiating a refresh, it is important to note all relevant configuration settings in the sandbox environment, such as Single Sign-On (SSO) configurations, Subscriptions, etc. This makes it easier to re-enter the necessary details and restore integrations once the environment is refreshed.

In addition, some setup steps can be completed once in Production, so they are carried over automatically during future refreshes, helping to reduce manual effort. For example, End Points can be created in Production with the Environment set to Beta. Also, Test End Point and Test Credential details can also be entered directly as part of the Event Driven Configuration setup, allowing test connections to be prepared in advance.

Configuration Manager Access

Configuration Managers are super users who access integration, system, and maintenance tools through the Self-Service Dashboard. To re-establish integrations in the sandbox, your organization must already have active Configuration Manager users. Each Configuration Manager should have a linked user account with username/password access enabled, as the sandbox will be refreshed with a slice of Production data which will wipe any sandbox-specific SSO configuration. Enabling this login method ensures the user can access the sandbox post-refresh without assistance from SAP Fieldglass Support.

For guidance on creating or updating Configuration Manager users, check out the SAP Help Portal.

Single Sign-On (SSO)

Single Sign-On allows users to log into SAP Fieldglass with their corporate credentials. If your Production environment uses SSO and you want similar access in the sandbox, you will need to recreate your SSO configuration so users can authenticate successfully in your test environment. To do this, navigate to the Single Sign-On tile on the Self-Service Dashboard to re-enter your test details.

You can also update any connector fields that rely on SSO deep links as required.

  • Watch the Learn how to Manage the Single Sign-on (SSO) Certificate and Learn how to Manage the Columns on a Connector, Add Native Fields, and Add and Combine Custom Fields Configuration Manager Tutorial videos for more information.

Event Driven Transactions

Event Driven Transactions enable real time communication between SAP Fieldglass and your other systems. If Event Driven Transactions are used in your Production environment, they will need to be recreated in the sandbox for successful integration testing. Without updating test details after a refresh, the transactions from the sandbox will not reach your test applications.

Use the Event Driven Configuration and Punchout Accounts tiles on the Self-Service Dashboard to re-enter the required test details.

  • Watch the Manage Event Driven Configs and Punchout Accounts Configuration Manager Tutorial video for more information.

Note: If Custom Scripts are utilized in Event Driven Configs, they need to be re-enabled and associated to the Buyer account after a refresh. Submit a case via SAP for Me or the SAP Fieldglass Help Center for support.

Subscriptions

If your company utilizes Subscriptions and they need to be re-enabled in the sandbox, navigate to the Integration Subscriptions tile on the Self-Service dashboard to open the Subscription List page to make updates.

  • Watch the Learn how to Create Integration Subscriptions Configuration Manager Tutorial video for step-by-step instructions.

The End Point defines the properties of the server that SAP Fieldglass must communicate with to push or pull connector files using a subscription. Following a refresh, make sure the End Point details are accurate if they need to be utilized.

  • Watch the Learn how to Create an End Point Configuration Manager Tutorial video for steps on how to setup End Points.

Configuration Managers can also create and manage the company's different types of security assets using the Encryption Keys and Certificates tile on the Self-Service Dashboard.

  • Watch the Learn how to Create Webservice and License Keys Configuration Manager Tutorial for more information.

Summary

By following this checklist and leveraging our short Configuration Manager tutorial videos, you can quickly and efficiently re-establish integrations in your sandbox environment post-refresh.

Don’t forget to explore the SAP Help Portal for more information on the SAP Fieldglass Sandbox Environment Landscape.

entry.title: Re: BMH - 'Create Stock Transport' button with retail sites
entry.content:
Dear @MorganeleaB,

In Public Cloud for Retail a site is essentially the retail term for plant/location: Plant, Location = Site, so the way to make it work with sites is ensure your sites have the required plant + BP assignments that the app checks

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-retail/maintaining-sites-for-retail-3i3-

The Create Stock Transport Order button in Transfer Stock Cross Plant (F1957) is plant-based and SAP explicitly checks plant prerequisites customer/supplier assignment for the issuing plant and a default purchasing organization for the receiving plant

Summary:

If in your tenant/setup the Retail site is not represented as a plant usable in those SSCUIs or you’re prevented from maintaining the plant shipping/purchasing-org assignments then F1957 one-click STO won’t activate because it’s explicitly checking plant-based prerequisites like you saw in SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2891646

In that case the standard workaround is to create the STO (UB) via the purchasing apps instead of one-click in F1957. The BMH process itself is simply STO type UB between issuing/receiving plants

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/experiencing-supply-chain-business-scenarios-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/exploring-the-stock-transfer-without-delivery-bmh-scenario

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Hide / Show dimensions on Tables and Charts in SAC in view / present mode
entry.content:
Hello @Raghav_S 

First and foremost - I absolutely apologize for such a late answer (was extremely busy during Q4/2025 and barely had a time for contribution in community blogs).

To your question. If you would like to have empty table initially (with no dimensions), first you would need to manually remove all dimensions rows in your table's structure on a design time. 

Then, you would need to modify initialization script, depicted in Step3, so that all elements (i.e. dimensions) in drop-down widget are populated as though they are all invisible from the beginning, in other words all they should have suffix (HIDDEN) in their name.

Since your table/chart won't have any dimension defined, you would need to populate them not from the table/chart, but manually.

Script in Step3 should look like below:

// reset global dimensions collections
while (actualDimensionIdTable.length) { actualDimensionIdTable.pop(); }  // dimensions id
while (actualDimensionDescTable.length) { actualDimensionDescTable.pop(); }  // dimensions description

// Manually populate Dimensions
var tableDimensionList = [
	"SALES_ORG", 
	"SALES_ORG_ID", 
	"PARTNER_ID", 
	"DELIVERY_STATUS"
];
// Manually populate Dimensions Description
var modelDimensionDescList = [
	"Sales Org (HIDDEN)",
	"Sales Org Id (HIDDEN)",
	"Partner Id (HIDDEN)",
	"Delivery Status (HIDDEN)"
];

// Create collections
for (i = 0; i < tableDimensionList.length; i++) {
	actualDimensionIdTable.push(tableDimensionList[i]);
	actualDimensionDescTable.push(modelDimensionDescList[i]);
}

// Populate Show/Hide dropdown list
for (var i = 0; i < actualDimensionIdTable.length; i++) {
	dropdownDimensionTable.addItem (
		actualDimensionDescTable[i]
	);
}

Note, all dimensions and their description above have to be named exactly how they are defined in your model.

Hope this helps out.

Best regards,

Slavek Kapitanec

entry.title: 2H2025 - SFCMP-31209 - Advanced Filters on Worksheets - "Unable to load data" error
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We are experiencing an issue with the new Advanced Filters on Worksheets functionality. A Second Manager has created more than 35 filters on a worksheet. As a result, the list of filters and the Manage button are no longer available, and the user receives the following error message:
"Unable to load data. Check your internet connection."

According to the SAP Implementation Guide "You can have up to 30 criteria per filter spread across one or multiple conditions." 
But this limitation refers to the number of criteria per filter, not the total number of filters.

I've reproduced this issue in our Preview instance. It appears that the system response time increases as more filters are created, and when the number exceeds 35, the system fails to load the filters, resulting in the error above.

For Executive Review, filter data is stored in Metadata Framework (MDF) objects, and filters can be deleted via Manage Data > CompFilterDefinition by entering the filter ID. However, MDF export doesn't contain worksheet advanced filters, they are not stored in CompFilterDefinition, so we are unable to delete or manage them through MDF.

Question:

  • Where are worksheet advanced filter definitions stored, and is there a way to manage or delete them outside of the user interface?

Any guidance or experience with this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks, Antanina

entry.title: Re: API_PURCHASEORDER – “Document contains no items” when...
entry.content:
Dear @dwix57,

That error is misleading in this case. With API_PURCHASEORDER_2 (OData V4) in Public Cloud SAP currently supports only:

  • PO type NB (Standard) and
  • customer PO types copied from NB (typically ZNB) with all dependent customizing

If you send another document type like NBF2 and even SAP-delivered variants like NB2/NBF2 the API can end up returning 06/010 Document contains no items although your JSON clearly contains items

SAPs Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3698875

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3555741

Proposal:

If you truly want a supplier Returns PO not an STO:

  • Use PurchaseOrderType = NB or a ZNB by copy of NB
  • Keep IsReturnsItem = true on the item
  • Do not send SupplyingPlant that’s STO semantics and often indicates you’re trying to do a plant-to-plant process

Example:

{

  "PurchaseOrderType": "NB",

  "Supplier": "110001",

  "CompanyCode": "BMII",

  "PurchasingOrganization": "MGPO",

  "PurchasingGroup": "001",

  "DocumentCurrency": "PHP",

  "_PurchaseOrderItem": [

    {

      "Material": "10",

      "Plant": "02BP",

      "OrderQuantity": 5,

      "PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit": "PAC",

      "IsReturnsItem": true,

      "NetPriceAmount": 1,

      "NetPriceQuantity": 1,

      "OrderPriceUnit": "PAC"

    }

  ]

}

If you must differentiate returns POs by document type for downstream logic then create a custom PO type copied from NB (ZNB*) and use that in the API

Because you’re using SupplyingPlant, this looks like an STO-style process even if your business labels it PO with supplier. The Purchase Order API is known to be restricted to NB/copies and historically not suitable for STO scenarios

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3110493

In Public Cloud, SAP provides a separate Stock Transport Order OData V4 API

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/807b2c79e22c4ef7a4c30c928bb3344e.html

So if the real intent is store plant ->warehouse plant stock transfer, you should switch to that STO API rather than forcing it through API_PURCHASEORDER_2

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.content:
Hi all, first of all – thanks so much for learning with SAP and engaging in the SAP Community.

As rightfully said through our support channels, the detailed score report is not available yet and no exact timeline can be communicated at this point. However, you will soon be able to see your own score compared to the cut score. Finally, for the Generative AI Developer exam, the cut score is currently still at 100% which makes it much harder to pass. This was the case for all the pilots published last November. We've already reviewed the exam with independent experts and will implement a lower cut score soon. 

BTW: We verify the task completion via API, which is why it's such a fast response :).

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Decommissioning solution manager
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are in the process for migrating our sap system to S/4Hana public could . The project has already started .

Currently the solution manager 7.2  on our landscape is running on windows server and  with Oracle 12 C database . The database is already out of support .

We are not using any of the solution manager functionalities . And hence would like to decommission this solution manager system.

Could you please confirm if there is any issue or requirement from SAP to have it running ?

Given that the new S/4 HANA implementation is already in progress.

Thanks.

 




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Decommissioning solution manager
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

There is no mandatory SAP requirement to keep SAP Solution Manager 7.2 running for an SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition implementation.

For S/4HANA Cloud (public), SAP’s recommended lifecycle management tool is SAP Cloud ALM. Solution Manager is not required to operate or support S/4HANA Cloud systems.

If Solution Manager is not being used (e.g. ChaRM, Test Management, Monitoring, Maintenance Planner) and there are no remaining dependencies from ECC or other SAP systems, it can be safely decommissioned.

Before decommissioning, please ensure:

  • No active SAP systems depend on Solution Manager

  • Any required historical data (audit/compliance) is archived or exported

Given that ECC systems will be decommissioned after the project, there is no SAP-mandated need to keep Solution Manager running.

If I can be of any further assistance to you regarding your query, please return this case to me for further analysis or else kindly confirm this case at your convenience.

if you’re satisfied with the support received, I would appreciate it if you could take a moment to complete the short survey that will be sent after the case is closed. This survey specifically reflects your interaction with me and the support I provided. Your feedback is valuable to both SAP and myself, and it helps us improve our service.

Thank you again for your time and collaboration.

Kind Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private - What's New in Q1 2026
entry.content:
Happy 2026 SAP Universe!

Welcome to a new year. We’re excited to kick things off with the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01 release, arriving this February. This update brings a rich set of new features and enhancements across the SAP Cloud ERP suite—furthering our commitment to helping you run your business with more efficiency, intelligence, and compliance.

As with every new release, there is a wealth of fresh information, documentation, and enablement resources to explore. The good news? The release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private is already in the process of updating all content to reflect the 2025 FPS01 release. This update will be fully completed during the week of February 27th, and no action is required on your part. SAP will handle everything for you.

 

Access the release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private

 

Tool Overview

The release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private, delivered through SAP Enterprise Support, is an essential resource for end users and administrators alike. It simplifies how you explore and manage new release content by providing:

  • Curated access to relevant documentation and tools
  • Guided insights tailored to your functional needs
  • Enablement opportunities, including webinars, workshops, and training, designed to help you make the most of each release

Whether you're responsible for system administration or simply want to understand what’s new, the release navigator is your one-stop destination for staying informed.

 

Content Insights

To help you quickly find what matters most, here’s a snapshot of the new and continuously updated content aligned with the 2025 FPS01 release. If you have suggestions for future topics, we’d love to hear from you in the comments.

 

New Content

Webinar Series page
We’re pleased to introduce a new dedicated page within the release navigator that highlights all upcoming and past SAP‑hosted webinars. This centralized resource makes it easier than ever to find the most relevant content for your targeted release.

entry.title: Re: C4C Workflow Calculation for opportunities not working
entry.content:
Hi colleague,

You can also approach it in the following way. If what you need is to know the number of days spent in a phase, you can handle this in the date definitions: as long as a date is greater than the Sales Phase Start Date, the incremental value is maintained, as shown in Image A.

Then, at the action level, you can define an incremental counter, as illustrated in Image B. After that, you can create another rule to reset the date field to blank, so the condition of the first rule is no longer met and the incremental calculation stops.

AB

I hope this helps.
Best regards.

Fernando DJ Espitia.

entry.title: Dimension is not populating in POP UP
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I am working in SAP Analytics Cloud – Analytics Designer. 

Requirement 

I created a Popup with a CheckboxGroup to select City (dimension) values. 
On clicking Save, the selected cities should filter a table. 

Popup Script 

var selectedCities = CheckboxGroup_2.getSelectedKeys(); 
 
if (selectedCities.length === 0) { 
   Utils.setCityFilter([]); 
} else { 
   Utils.setCityFilter(selectedCities); 
} 
 
Popup_1.close(); 
 

Utility Function 

export function setCityFilter(cities: string[]): void { 
   var ds = Table_2.getDataSource(); 
   ds.setDimensionFilter("City", cities); 
} 

 
ONINITIALIZATION: 

    var ds = Table_2.getDataSource(); 

  

    // Get City members (SAC syntax) 

    var members = ds.getMembers("City"); 

  

    for (var j = 0; j < members.length; j++) { 

  

        CheckboxGroup_2.addItem( 

            members[j].id, 

            members[j].description 

        ); 

  

        ALL_CITIES.push(members[j].id); 

    } 

  

    // Select all by default 

    CheckboxGroup_2.setSelectedKeys(ALL_CITIES); 

     

  

CheckboxGroup_2.addItem(members[j].id ,members[j].description); 

POPUP_1 Code: 

var selectedCities = CheckboxGroup_2.getSelectedKeys(); 

  

if (selectedCities.length === 0) { 

    Utils.setCityFilter([]); 

} else { 

    Utils.setCityFilter(selectedCities); 

} 

Popup_1.close(); 

 

 

 

Issue 

  • Checkbox values are selected correctly 
  • Script is executed 
  • But the table does not update 
  • City dimension is already present in the Filters panel (City – All) 

Question 

  1. Why does setDimensionFilter() not work when the dimension is already in the Filters panel? 
  1. What is the correct way to apply a dimension filter via popup checkbox in this case? 
  1. Should ds.getFilters().setDimensionFilter() be used instead? 

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.content:

If not, are post-invoice cost changes expected to be handled outside the sales order through financial postings and reporting only?

Also, while non-financial fields (for example PO number or texts) can be changed after billing, is it supported or recommended to manually adjust sales order line item pricing or quantities to reflect updated COGS and margin?

entry.title: SAP Macros Table - Get Selected Table Row Data
entry.content:
Hello. I am trying to retrieve the data from a table row of my SAP Macros table(sap.fe.macros.Table). Application is running a odata V4 using the Flexible Programming Model. I was able to read some SAP documentation on the getObject() function. When I use this, it seems to only retrieve the data from visible columns, but not hidden ones. Is that by design? Or is there another way I can retrieve everything from the row? 

For Example:

Columns A-D are visible in my application, and oSelectedData = {1, 2, 3, 4}. So only the visible columns. Columns E-H are hidden, and the values are not returned. I was expecting to have {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8}. 

ABCDEFGH
12345678
//Grab Table object
const oTable = this.byId("Table");

//Get selected contexts
const oSelectedContexts = oTable.getSelectedContexts();
const oContext = oSelectedContexts[0];

//Get table row data 
const oSelectedData = oSelectedContexts[0].getObject();

entry.title: Re: How do we trigger a Success Alert.
entry.content: For success scenarios, the cleanest approach is usually to trigger the alert at the end of the main iFlow rather than forcing an exception. If you can add a final step before the End Message, you can call your generic email iFlow via Process Direct there, as long as it’s in the same tenant. It’s not as obvious as error handling, but it avoids artificial failures. I document patterns like this in Phonexa so I don’t have to rethink them every time a similar integration comes up.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud – Extensibility for Supplier Rebates (2R7) Business Volume Selection Criteria
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (scope item 2R7 – Supplier Rebates / Condition Contracts) and need guidance on extensibility options for Business Volume Selection Criteria.

Requirement

  1. Our customer wants to drive rebate eligibility/rates based on a field that exists in Product (Material) Master as a custom field (created via in-app extensibility).
  2. Conceptually, they want this field available for:
    1. Business Volume Selection Criteria (field / field combination), so the agreement can filter volumes based on that attribute (in addition to standard fields like supplier/material/material group, etc.) Material Group is already in use for another filter.

Questions

  1. In Public Cloud, is it possible to extend Business Volume Selection Criteria (add new fields or new field combinations) for supplier rebates / condition contracts?
  2. If the answer is “not directly”, is there any supported way to implement custom logic to read Product Master custom fields during business volume determination?
  3. For example via BAdI, enhancement spot, or another officially supported extensibility option that can influence the business volume selection/determination.
  4. If there is no supported extension for this, what is the recommended standard approach (mapping the attribute to an available standard field like product hierarchy/material group, or restructuring master data/pricing logic) while staying within clean-core principles?

Thanks,

Ezequiel

entry.title: Customer Objects Deep Dive in RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard
entry.content:
Gain deeper insights into your customer objects

We are more than happy to announce that we have recently released the Customer Objects deep dive dashboard, a powerful new addition to the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard that significantly enhances the extensibility-related KPIs displayed in the System View.

The Customer Objects deep dive dashboard provides detailed information about customer objects in eligible systems and empowers customers to analyze the clean core compliance of these objects in more depth and with more clarity.

It can be used both as a standalone analytical tool accessible through the RISE with SAP Methodology tab on the SAP Cloud ALM launchpad and as an integrated component of the System View within the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. When accessed through the System View, the data is automatically filtered for the specific system and selected clean core level, providing a seamless analytical experience. The data is based on the most recent ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) check result that was imported for the selected system into the System View dashboard.

How ABAP Test Cockpit supports clean core level concept

 

The dashboard provides table views for both customer objects and their referenced SAP objects, thus offering comprehensive visibility into the relationship between custom code and (standard) SAP functionality.

The Customer Objects table view includes details about a customer object, such as:

  • Development Package assignment
  • Object Name and Type
  • Clean Core Level
  • Technical Debt Score
  • Number of findings per clean core level (B,C,D)
Customer Objects table view

 

Clicking on an entry in the list of customer objects allows you to drill deeper and display details about the SAP objects that are referenced by the selected customer object.

This view provides details such as

  • Metadata
  • Technical Debt Share, i.e. the extent to which a customer object contributes to the overall technical debt score of the selected system
  • Distribution of findings and their categories (Reference-based, Behavior-based)
  • Classification of the referenced SAP object (Classic API, Internal SAP object, Not recommended SAP object)

Referenced SAP Objects table view

 

Call to Action

RISE customers should begin exploring the Customer Objects dashboard immediately to gain insights into their current extensibility landscape and develop strategies for maintaining clean core compliance throughout their transformation journey.

 

Additional Resources:

Customer Objects | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE Internals
entry.content:
Wow, really nice.

Unfortunately, I think some things are out of date now.  For example, in Sybase ASE Architecture Foundation, it says:

"All resources are located in a single Shared Memory Segment, allocated, and usually locked • which is shared by the Engines (Unix Processes)"

Is describing the old "process mode" for the ASE kernel.  The new "threaded mode" uses native OS threads.  Internally ASE creates "engines" which are really just thread queues mimicking the old process based engines.   Threaded mode makes output from monitoring tools like sp_sysmon weird because none of the "engines" (thread queues) are ever waiting for I/O (a ASE "process" (really a thread) goes idle waiting for I/O, but sp_sysmon doesn't show that)

I'm not sure where to look to see how much of the time a user process was waiting for I/O to complete

entry.title: Guidance Needed – Repartitioning HANA Table /BIC/FZIM_C12
entry.content:
We would like your guidance regarding repartitioning one of our large tables in SAP CAR on PERSON_NAME. The table /BIC/FZ***  is currently split into 4 partitions, and we are seeing that partition ID 4 contains approximately 1.7 billion records and is rapidly approaching the 2 billion record limit in PERSON_NAME. Due to this, we are considering repartitioning the table, however we are not sure what the correct partitioning strategy should be.

At present, the table is partitioned using two layers: Round-Robin as the first layer and Range partitioning as the second layer based on the column KEY_*** (ranges 0,1,2). The data distribution appears to be highly skewed, with most records in partition 4 and significantly fewer in the other partitions (partition 1 being the smallest), so we are unsure whether the partitioning was originally done correctly and whether this strategy is appropriate for this table going forward.

We have reviewed SAP Notes such as DIGITS (BW-related) and DIGITS (general PERSON_NAME partitioning recommendations), and we are looking for a recommendation similar to the table-specific guidance mentioned in Question 27 of SAP Note DIGITS where the partition method and column are clearly defined for specific tables.

Could you please advise whether there are any table-specific recommendations for how /BIC/FZ***  should be partitioned, and what partition method and key column(s) would be best to avoid future growth issues?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Guidance Needed – Repartitioning HANA Table /BIC/FZIM...
entry.content:
For the issue that table is reaching to 2 billion limited, and this can be handled from HANA DB side.

You could review this note below and perform a reorg for the large table as mentioned in note:

2019973 - Handling Very Large Data Volumes in SAP BW on SAP HANA
2374652 - Handling Very Large Data Volumes in SAP BW/4HANA

-> Handling Large Data Volumes - 2 Billion Limit Value Reached

As a remind that you need apply file TABLE_PLACEMENT.zip before the reorg as mentioned in this note.

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE Internals
entry.content:
> Wow, really nice.

Thank you.

> I think some things are out of date now

The document was published 13 years ago, yes, but the information is not out of date.

> ... describing the old "process mode" ...

  1. The document is structured and incremental, one needs to understand the Process Kernel first,  in order to properly understand the Threaded Kernel.  You need to read past page 1.  
  2. The applicable ASE version is stated at the bottom right of each page
  3. Go to page 8 for the Threaded Kernel.

Your further comments are about ASE, not the subject document.

> Internally ASE creates "engines" which are really just thread queues mimicking the old process based engines.

No. 

  • In both modes, ASE uses o/s Threads, but do not let that confuse you
  • In Threaded mod, ASE runs as a single (unix) process,
    • with the Threads exposed and named properly such that you can monitor them
  • In Process Mode, ASE does not "use threads to mimic engines",
    • ASE runs one (unix) process per Engine
    • with the o/s threads quite internal (for a simplified understanding only, you can conceptualise that as one o/s thread per Engine, but it is a different Mode, it is not running in Threaded Mode and mimicking Engines).

> Threaded mode makes output from monitoring tools like sp_sysmon weird because none of the "engines" (thread queues) are ever waiting for I/O (a ASE "process" (really a thread) goes idle waiting for I/O, 

Much confusion.

  • In Threaded Mode, ASE does not have Engines, it has Engine Threads and I/O Threads. 
  • Yes, Engine Threads never wait for an I/O, that is by design. 
    • I/O Threads wait for I/O to complete
    • Do not look for I/O issues in Engine Threads, or Engine issues in I/O Threads
  • an ASE "process" is an internal concept, a spid, it is not an o/s thread or ASE Thread,  It is properly named Task in my document. 
    • Do not confuse ASE Threads or o/s threads with ASE internal Tasks, they have northing to do with each other, there is no equivalence
  • an ASE Task (spid) does not "go idle" while waiting for I/O to complete, it goes "waiting for I/O to complete" (sp_who = "sleeping")
  • an ASE Task (spid) "goes idle" when it has not sent a batch to ASE, when it is not waiting for anything from ASE, ie. ASE is waiting to receive a batch from the Task (sp_who = "recv sleep")
  • In my document page 9, see if you can differentiate:
    • ASE = single unix process, dark grey 3D square
    • ASE Engine Thread Pool = blue 3D squares
    • ASE I/O Thread Pool = blue 3D squares
    • ASE internal Task (spid)
      • Run Queue = blue 3D spheres
      • Sleep Queue = red 3D spheres

> but sp_sysmon doesn't show that)

Sysmon does show that, in the I/O Thread Pool (syb_system_pool) metrics, not in the Engine Thread Pool (syb_default_pool) metrics.  I have no problem at all reading and understanding sysmon metrics, but one needs to examine the overall metrics, over time, and aggregate and display the metrics in a meaningful way (see below).

Sysmon provides ASE (server level) metrics, not Task level metrics.

Cheers
Derek

entry.title: Re: Single Freight Order with Multiple stages
entry.content:
Here is what it needs to be done: 

  1. Create two zones Zone A, Zone B 
  2. Add A (usually shipping point) to Zone A
  3. Add B , C, D locations to Zone B
  4. Create Transportation Lane between Zone A and Zone B
  5. Create Intrazone on Zone B.
  6. Now plan your FUs, system should create one freight order with multiple stages.

entry.title: Derivation of commitment item from real internal order does not work when creating ME31K
entry.content:
Hi all experts,

I have an issue with FM Derivation Strategies that needs support.

In the FMDERIVE t-code, I have already entered four rules in the following order:

  1. Function module FMDT_READ_MD_ASSET for reading asset master data

  2. Cost center assigns commitment item

  3. Internal order assigns commitment item

  4. Asset number range assigns commitment item

I created an asset assigned to an internal order, but when I created a contract in ME31K, the commitment item was updated according to rule number 4, not the commitment item assigned in rule 3.

The FM trace showed that the system skipped rule 3 because it did not find any values (although I entered a valid internal order in the asset master data) and derived the commitment item from rule 4 instead.

However, when I tested with a cost center in the asset master data, the system correctly derived the commitment item from the cost center itself, not from the asset number range.

If anyone has encountered and resolved this issue, please explain it to me.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Key Supplier Account Challenges and Resolutions in SAP Ariba Network
entry.content:
Dear SAP Users,

Supplier account issues in SAP Ariba Network are more common than expected and can significantly impact business operations. Based on real-world experience, this article outlines frequently encountered scenarios and shares practical solutions to help organizations manage suppliers more effectively.

Organizations that manage supplier registration, qualification, modular questionnaires, purchase orders, order confirmations, inbound deliveries, service entry sheets, goods receipts, and invoicing through the SAP Ariba Network often encounter challenges when working with suppliers. Many of these challenges arise from issues related to supplier account management. Supplier account challenges can delay sourcing and transactions in SAP Ariba Network. 

Based on my experience, I have identified some common scenarios frequently faced by organizations, along with the appropriate solutions to address them.

Scenarios:

  • Supplier account suspended due to non-payment of SAP Ariba Network (SMP) fees, with the supplier requesting to create a new account
  • Supplier administrator has left the organization
  • Supplier Registration/Sourcing Event sent to user who is not the correct contact to respond to the questionnaire
  • Supplier has multiple ANID used for several customers

Addressing these issues proactively can help minimize delays, improve supplier collaboration, and ensure smoother transaction processing within SAP Ariba Network.

 

  • Supplier account suspended due to non-payment of SAP Ariba Network (SMP) fees, with the supplier requesting to create a new account

 

Supplier's action

IT's action

Supplier Relation team's action

Impact on existing POs, Event?

Supplier to create a new Ariba Account

1. Unlink the user ids' and AN ID in Ariba Strategic sourcing.
2. Update the ERP ID with the new AN ID mapping in business network

1. Add the new email ID

2. Reinvite the supplier for registration on new email id

 

 

 

To be processed offline

 

 

 

 

 

 

Advantage of doing this is that the existing response of Supplier registration will move to the new ANID and now new POs can flow to the new ANID.

 

  • Supplier administrator has left the organization

Supplier shall follow the below steps -

Step 1: Go to url -https://service.ariba.com/Supplier.aw/109526060/aw?awh=r&awssk=x8h3R2SG&dard=1 and click on ‘Help’ as shown below

 

 

Step 2: From the help topics that appears, click “Support”

 

Step 3: New window will open and click on ‘Contact Support’ as below

 

 

Step 4: Type ‘I need to reassign the administrator account’ and click on ‘Next’

 

Step 5: Under “Is the current administrator still with your company?,” click “No.”

 

Step 6: Under “Do you have access to retrieve emails sent to the listed administrator’s email address?” click “No.”

 

Step 7: Make note of the items Ariba will ask for as part of the reassignment – these are important as these are what SAP uses to identify that you are a legitimate contact for the company, for account security.

  • Company Name:
  • ANID:
  • Current administrator name:
  • Current administrator email address:
  • First and last name of caller:
  • New administrator name:
  • New administrator email:
  • Phone number:

Step 8: Enter the details in section ‘confirm the details’ and click ‘Next’ as below

 

Step 9: Provide the mandatory details in ‘Contact options’ and click on ‘Submit’

 

 

  • Supplier Registration/Sourcing Event sent to user who is not the correct contact to respond to the questionnaire

 

Supplier's action

IT's action

Supplier Relation team's action

Impact on existing POs, Event?

New user to create new account with registration invite link

NA

Reinvite the supplier to new user email id

NA

 

  • Supplier has multiple ANID used for several customers

To optimize navigation between multiple accounts, supplier can link user IDs and switch easily between accounts, by following the steps below:

In the upper-right corner of the application, click [user initials] > Link User IDs.

  • If the username and password for the account to be linked is known:

    1. Enter the login information under the No Approval Needed heading.
    2. Click Link Accounts.
  • If the username and password for the account to be linked is not known:

    1. Enter the username for the user account you wish to link to under the Approval Needed heading.
    2. Click Send link request.

      • This will send an email to that user with the option to accept your link request.

In order to switch to the user once it has been linked:

  1. In the upper-right corner of the application, click [user initials] > Switch Account.
  2. Click the name of the account holder. User must click the [user name] in order to switch to the user that has been linked. If user clicks anywhere else, it will not switch successfully.

 

Conclusion

Suppliers often are not aware about how to resolve any issues they encounter, having a repository with solution will always help the organization for a smooth day to day operations like minimizing delays, reducing manual intervention, and maintaining continuity in SAP Ariba Network transactions.

entry.title: SAPUI5 – Pagination in Table on button click using S/4 Hana cloud oData service
entry.content:
Hi, in this blog I’m going to explain the pagination in Table with S/4 Hana cloud oData service with flexibility to control number of visible rows per page. 

 

In this example, I am using S/4 Hana cloud oData service through destination. I have 621 records in Product entity set and by default, I have to display 10 records per click and user can change number of visible rows. So, I have placed four nav buttons for First page, Previous page, Next page and Last page with page count information and drop down for number of visible rows and then some magical code.

 

Firstly, create new front end web and mobile application with zproducts name.

 

In the canvas, give title as Products and remove bottom text.

 

Add Integration:

Go to Integrations tab and select add integration and then select BTP destinations under sap systems.

In BTP destinations, select our S/4 Hana cloud service destination i.e., S4HANA_Joule_Product and select A_Product entityset. Now Select install integration and enable data entity. You can browse real data by selecting browse real data. Click on save at top right corner.

 

Add Variables:

Now go to Variables tab and go to page variables and select add page variable and choose from scratch.

Add variable noOfVisibleRows with value type Number with initial value 10.

Similarly add variables totalNoOfRows with value type Number,

Variable currentPage with value type Number with initial value 1,

totalPages with value type Number with initial value 1 and

variable isRefresh with value type true/false with initial value false and Click on save at top right corner.

User Interface:

In User Interface, we install table with data adaptor from marketplace and assign our entity set to it to fetch the data and we will add new property to make table to load the data at each time when user clicks on nav buttons or change number of visible rows.

Go to User Interface tab and select marketplace and search for basic table with data adapter and install it. Drag and drop the basic table with data adapter to canvas and select configure and select A_product and add the required columns and in properties tab, at pagination in Object with properties, assign noOfVisibleRows page variable to page size, assign currentPage page variable to Page Number and click on save. Click on save and exit and save at top right corner.

Go to open in component template editor at bottom in properties tab and select properties and in properties, add new property Refresh of type true/false. Go to view and select add logic to Basic table with data adapter at bottom right corner. Drag and drop Receive event and select Internal property 'Refresh' changed and connect end of the receive event to start of get record collection. Click on save and exit. You can see Refresh property in properties tab.

 

 

Assign Refresh property with isRefresh page variable

 

Now select at page level and open logic canvas. Drag and drop record collection and connect to Page Mounted and assign A_Product to resource and for pagination make Include total count as true by selecting binding type as static values true/false.

 

Now drag and drop set page variable and select variable name as totalNoOfRows and assign formula outputs["Get record collection"].totalCount for value and connect end of get Record Collection top node to start of Set Page Variable node.

Similarly, drag and drop another set page variable for totalPages and assign formula CEIL(outputs["Get record collection"].totalCount / pageVars.noOfVisibleRows).

Add Nav Buttons:

Now drag and drop container at bottom of table and select layout as horizontal.

Drag and drop button inside the container and name it as First. For disabled, add formula IF(pageVars.currentPage > 1, false, true) and in logic convas, add two set page variables. One for currentPage and set the value to 1 and another for isRefresh and assign formula NOT(pageVars.isRefresh).

Similarly add another button inside the container and name it as Previous. For disabled, add formula IF(pageVars.currentPage > 1, false, true) and in logic convas, add two set page variables. One for currentPage and assign formula pageVars.currentPage – 1 and another for isRefresh and assign formula NOT(pageVars.isRefresh).

Add another button inside the container and name it as Next. For disabled, add formula IF(pageVars.currentPage < pageVars.totalPages, false, true) and in logic convas, add two set page variables. One for currentPage and assign formula pageVars.currentPage + 1 and another for isRefresh and assign formula NOT(pageVars.isRefresh).

Add another button inside the container and name it as Last. For disabled, add formula IF(pageVars.currentPage < pageVars.totalPages, false, true) and in logic convas, add two set page variables. One for currentPage and assign formula pageVars. totalPages and another for isRefresh and assign formula NOT(pageVars.isRefresh).

Drag and drop text after Previous button and for content add formula pageVars.currentPage + " / " + pageVars.totalPages.

Drag and drop Dropdown and give label text as No of Rows and select option list -> List of values and add value and give values 10, 25, 50, 100 and save. Make Placeholder text as empty.

In Logic pane of dropdown field, drag and drop set page variable and assign variable name to noOfVisibleRows and add formula IF(self.value, NUMBER(self.value), pageVars.noOfVisibleRows ) for value.

Similarly, drag and drop another page variable and assign currentpageno to it and value as 1.

Another page variable with totalpages and formula for value is CEIL(pageVars.totalNoOfRows / pageVars.noOfVisibleRows) and save at the top right corner.

Add another page variable with isRefresh with formula for value NOT(pageVars.isRefresh).

Preview Application:

Save at top right corner and click on preview and select open web preview. Now, you can see our application with pagination and flexibility to control number of visible rows per page.

 

 

By doing this, we can easily add pagination in Table on button click by consuming S/4 Hana cloud oData service with flexibility to control number of visible rows per page. 

 

That’s it!!!

entry.title: Re: Customer Name range
entry.content:
/* ===========================
Check This
=========================== */

DECLARE FromCardName NVARCHAR(254);
DECLARE ToCardName NVARCHAR(254);

DECLARE FromSGrp NVARCHAR(254);
DECLARE ToSGrp NVARCHAR(254);

/* --- Prompts --- */
FromCardName := '[%0]'; -- From Customer Name
ToCardName := '[%1]'; -- To Customer Name

FromSGrp := '[%2]'; -- From Sub Product Group
ToSGrp := '[%3]'; -- To Sub Product Group


SELECT
T0."DocNum",
T0."DocDate",
T0."CardCode",
T0."CardName",
T0."DocCur",
T3."ItmsGrpNam",
T5."Name" AS "Sub Product Group",
T4."Name" AS "Item Type",
'$' || T1."ItemCode" AS "Item Code",
T1."Dscription",
T1."WhsCode",
T1."Quantity" AS "PO Qty",
T1."OpenQty",
T1."unitMsr" AS "UOM",
T1."PriceBefDi" AS "Unit Price",
T1."DiscPrcnt" AS "% Disc",
T1."TotalFrgn" AS "Total Order Value",
T0."U_ETA",
T0."U_SHIPMODE",
T0."U_REASON"

FROM OPOR T0
INNER JOIN POR1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
INNER JOIN OITM T2 ON T1."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode"
INNER JOIN OITB T3 ON T2."ItmsGrpCod" = T3."ItmsGrpCod"
LEFT JOIN "@A_ITEMTYPE" T4 ON T2."U_ITEM_TYPES" = T4."Code"
LEFT JOIN "@A_SUBDIV" T5 ON T2."U_SUBDIV" = T5."Code"

WHERE
T0."DocStatus" = 'O'
AND T1."OpenQty" <> 0

/* ---- Customer Name From / To ---- */
AND (
(UPPER(T0."CardName") >= UPPER(FromCardName) OR FromCardName = '')
AND (UPPER(T0."CardName") <= UPPER(ToCardName) OR ToCardName = '')
)

/* ---- Sub Product Group From / To ---- */
AND (
(UPPER(IFNULL(T5."Code",'ZNA')) >= UPPER(FromSGrp) OR FromSGrp = '')
AND (UPPER(IFNULL(T5."Code",'ZNA')) <= UPPER(ToSGrp) OR ToSGrp = '')
)

ORDER BY
T0."CardName",
T0."DocNum";

entry.title: Hybrid Integration Pattern- SAP S/4HANA Vendor Data to Azure Service Bus Using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
I am currently working on a project where a Hybrid Integration platform (SAP BTP Integration Suite and Azure Integration Services) is used as middleware between SAP and Non-SAP applications.

Business Requirement:

Transmit new and modified Business Partner (vendor) details from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to Azure Service Bus using the hybrid integration setup.

In this first part of the blog, we explain how vendor messages generated from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SOAP Outbound APIs flow through the integration platform and Azure services.

Introduction:

In this project, we built an integration to send new and updated vendor (Business Partner) data from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to Azure Service Bus using SAP Integration Suite as the middleware.

SAP sends the vendor data as a SOAP message, which is processed and converted into XML format in SAP Integration Suite. The message is then published to Azure Service Bus, picked up by Azure Logic Apps, routed through Azure API Management, and finally delivered to the Coupa REST API endpoint.

This integration enables fast, secure, and reliable data exchange between SAP, Azure, and Coupa with minimal delay.

End to End Core Interface Design:

what I worked on as an SAP BTP Integration Suite Developer

Developed integrations between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SOAP Outbound APIs and Azure Service Bus using SAP Integration Suite iflows.

Implementation:

Step 1: Configure SOAP Sender Adapter

In this step, we set up the SOAP Sender Adapter so SAP Integration Suite can receive vendor data from SAP S/4HANA. We configure the URL, connect the WSDL, and enable security so SAP can safely send messages into the flow.

Step 2: Receive Vendor Data from SAP S/4HANA (SOAP)

It receives the Business Partner (Vendor) SOAP message sent from SAP S/4HANA and makes the XML payload available for processing.

Step 3: Add Tracking Details to the message

We use this step to track and identify each vendor message as it moves through the integration flow by adding values like vendor Number and Correlation ID.

Step 4: Decode Base64 Payload 

The Base64 Decoder is used to convert the encoded SAP payload into readable XML format. This ensures the data can be mapped and processed correctly in the integration flow.

Step 5: Map Vendor Source Data to Target Format

Here, we connect the SAP vendor fields to the matching fields in the target format so the data is passed correctly and can be understood by the next system.

Step 6:  Add Azure Message Headers

We add Azure-related details like MessageId, ContentType, and Label to the message. These help Azure Service Bus identify the message, track it properly, and process it correctly.

Step 7: Publish Vendor Data to Azure Service Bus Topic

In the step, we send the vendor data from SAP Integration Suite to Azure Service Bus. We connect securely to Azure and publish the message to a topic so it can be picked up and used by other systems.

Connection Tab:

Processing Tab:

What Happens After Data Reaches Azure Service Bus 

Once the vendor data is sent to the Azure Service Bus Topic, it is stored safely and made available for other systems to use. The topic  works like a message hub, where one message can be shared with multiple consumers if needed.

Azure Service Bus ensures reliable processing by handing message retries and moving failed messages to a dead-letter queue instead of losing them. This improves the reliability of integration and reduces rick data loss.

Another key benefit is system decoupling. SAP sends the message once, and downstream systems can process it independently. this improves performance and makes it easier to add new consumers in the future without changing the SAP integration flow.

Note:

In case error happen in the middle of the flow and from SAP, it is better a add a global retry mechanism so the message can be automatically retried. This is a recommended approach, but it is not part of the current blog.

 

In this hybrid integration, my role is to send the vendor data from SAP Integration Suite to Azure Service Bus, making sure the message is delivered safely so other system can pick it up process it further.

Conclusion:

This setup shows how SAP and Azure work together using SAP Integration Suite to send vendor data smoothly. By checking and converting the data before sending it to Azure Service Bus, the flow stays clean and avoids errors. Overall, this approach makes the integration easier to manage, more flexible, and able to handle growing business data needs.

entry.title: Fix pdfmake Table Width Issues with Long Continuous Text and Special Characters (SAP UI5)
entry.content:
Introduction
I recently hit a really specific edge case while building a PDF feature for our SAP UI5 app. We were using pdfmake, and honestly, everything looked great during standard testing. But then the backend sent over some real-world data—specifically these massive, continuous strings of text—and the whole layout just fell apart.
Even though we had set fixed column widths, the table completely ignored them. The text refused to wrap, spilled right over the borders, and basically ruined the document structure. It’s one of those issues you don't usually catch until you throw messy, non-standard data at your code. In this post, I’ll break down exactly why pdfmake struggles with this and the simple workaround we found to keep the layout intact without changing how the text looks.


Problem Description
We get a specific edge case while building a PDF feature in our SAP UI5 app using pdfmake(pdf playground). Everything looked fine until the backend sent over a massive string of text, which is  mix of special characters. Even though we had fixed-width columns, the table ignored them—text wouldn't wrap, it spilled over the cell borders, and basically broke the whole layout.

The issue became easier to reproduce when the input text had:
        Very long continuous strings
        No spaces
        A lot of special characters

Example:
ssssssssss@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@uuuuuuuuuuuuuiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiii

Even  noWrap: false was already set, it didn’t help. 

The reason became evident after studying the text analyzing logic of pdfmake.

When the text consists of a single, continuous word, pdfmake determines the whole length of the string from considering it as a single, unbreakable unit.

Both of the font issue and the PDFmake bug aren't the root cause of this. Wrap points can't be put into continuous text; additionally fonts like Roboto or NotoSans only aid in character rendering.

This will fix when we insert invisible wrap points into the string before sending it to pdfmake.

We used a zero-width space character that is (\u200B), which is invisible for human eye but allows PDF engines to wrap text safely

Code:

function safeBreak(str) {
  return (str || '').replace(/(.{1})/g, "$1\u200B");
}

 

This will not change the text looks in the PDF, but it gives pdfmake to break point to respect the table width and wrap content correctly.

The user can view the PDF in exactly the same manner. So as for pdfmake is properly wrap the text so the table width, this simply adds a few break points.

This turned out to be a invisible (for human eye) edge case when generating PDFs with dynamic data. Adding zero-width spaces solved the problem without affecting visible output.




Before Applying the Fix


After Applying the Fix

Try the Working Demo
If you want to see how this project, we put together a complete SAP UI5 sample app. It covers the basic pdfmake setup and shows exactly how to handle those long strings inside table cells so they don't break your layout. You can check out the code  here:  PDF_SAP 

Conclusion
Long, unbroken strings are definitely one of those hidden edges when you're generating PDFs with dynamic data. Since pdfmake needs explicit breakpoints to know when to wrap a line, it will break your table layout if it doesn't find them.
We went with the zero-width space (\u200B) in the end because, frankly, it just works. It makes the text wrap properly so the table widths doesn't get destroyed, but visually, you can't tell the difference, this annoying bug can be easily and safely fixed.

entry.title: Enable complex service Procurement in Ariba with service entry sheet export to SAP
entry.content:
Background:

Service Procurement refers to the process of acquiring services from external suppliers to meet the needs of an organization. Unlike products, which invoices purchasing physical products, service procurement focuses on obtaining intangible offerings that can range from consulting and legal services to cleaning and IT support. 

SAP Ariba procurement solutions provide businesses with a powerful suite of tools designed to address the complexities associated with service procurement.

Purpose: 

This functionality will enable users to create service sheets for purchase orders with the item category "Service" in Ariba Buying. Upon completion of the approval flow, the service sheet will be transferred to the S/4 system for validation and creation against the purchase order. Once the creation response is received from S/4 to Ariba through the CIG system, Ariba will then communicate the service sheet creation to the supplier through the SAP Business Network.

Configuration Steps in Ariba B&I

Step 1:   Request your DSC to submit a case to support asking for this feature to be enabled. 

  1. Create Service entry Sheet
  2. ExportServiceSheet
  3. Toggle SINV-5399 - Edit and resubmit the SES action for service sheet failed in external system due to ERP push error

Step 2:  Basic self-service parameters to be enabled in Intelligent configuration Manager

 

ParameterValueDescription
Application. Procure.EnableServiceitemFieldinRequsition YesAllows users to mark requisition line items as service line items
Application.Procure.EnableitemCategoryOnRequsitionLine YesSpecifies whether the item category option to mark line items as service items is displayed on the requisiton summary page
Application.ServiceSheet.EnableERPIntegrationYesSpecifies whether your site sends service sheets to the SAP ERP for approval. 
 Application.ServiceSheet.ServiceSheet ExternalApprovalEnabled  YesDetermines whether service sheets must undergo approval in SAP ERP before moving to the processes state in the buying solution
Application.ServiceSheet.AllowNonCatalogItem YesSpecifies whether suppliers can add non catalog items to service sheet in Ariba 
Application.ServiceSheet. EnableResendingOfFailedServiceSheetsToSBN YesDetermine whether the SAP Ariba solution initiates the resending of failed service sheets to SAP business network
Application.ServiceSheet.FixSESStatusIntegrationForCIGYesDetermine whether the status must be reset to the previous value for cancelled or force rejected service sheets that fail to export to SAP ERP from the Arina solution. 

Step 3: Enable below managed gateway for spend and Network Integration tasks. 

  1. Export Service Sheet Asynchronously
  2. Export Service Sheet Response to External System Asynchronously
  3. Import Service Sheet Approval Status
  4. Import Service Sheet Response Asynchronously
  5. Import Service Sheet Status Response Asynchronously

** Disable webservice format tasks in integration configuration as they might interfere with the export Behavior. 

Step 4: Add users to the below groups

  1. Service Sheet Entry User
  2. Service Sheet Editor 

Configuration Steps in SAP

Step 5: Enable below parameters in S4

SPRO-->SAP reference IMG-->Integration with Other SAP Components--> Managed Gateway for Spend&Network for Buyer-->SAP Business Network Integration-->Application Specific Settings -->Service Sheet-->Maintain Parameters for Service Sheet

Parameter in S4MandatoryDescription
ENTRYSHEET_CREATENoIf the purchase order at SAP ERP is in the edit mode, the system would not allow to create a service entry sheet. Configure the duration (in seconds) for which the system would attempt to create a service entry when the purchase order at ERP is in edit mode
ENABLE_SES_ITERATIONNo This configuration is only for SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing to avoid failure of the transaction document due to a locking issue while processing multiple service sheets for the same purchase order service type item

SPRO-->SAP reference IMG-->Integration with Other SAP Components--> Managed Gateway for Spend&Network for Buyer-->SAP Business Network Integration-->Application Specific Settings -->Service Sheet Response-->Maintain Parameters for Service Sheet Response

 

Parameter in S4MandatoryDescription
SES_RESPONSE_ENABLEDNoEnable or disable the Ariba provided enhancements for service sheet response

Step 6: Defining Output message control for service sheet in S4 System

SPRO-->SAP reference IMG-->Integration with Other SAP Components--> Managed Gateway for Spend&Network for Buyer-->SAP Business Network Integration-->Application Specific Settings -->Service Sheet Response-->Define Message Output Control for Service Sheet Response

Application: ES - Entry Sheet

Program: ARBCIG_ENTRYSHEET_UPDATE

FORM routine: SEND_ENTRYSHEET_RESPONSE

Step 7: Maintaining Event Linkages to Generate Service Sheet Responses

Create and activate the SES_RESPONSE event type linkage to generate service sheet responses after you revoke or delete service sheets

Step 8: BADI implementation related to S4 service entry sheet creation and approval. 

  • For auto approve SES in SAP - Implement BADI ARBCIG_SES_CREATE - Method UPDATE_SES_BAPITABLES - pass Service sheet header acceptance value 
  • To send approve status from S4 to Ariba - Implement BADI ARBCIG_SESR_UPDATE - method POST_CHANGE_SESR_DATA - using call function ARBCIG_SAP_RESPONSE_TO_P2P send header approval status in import pull  ServiceSheetImportPullRequest
  • For Ariba SES to be rejected outside financial posting period - Implement BADI ARBCIG_SES_DELETE - method UPDATE_SES_DELETE. Pass the posting date as SES rejection date (current date) 

Conclusion:

Enabling complex service procurement in Ariba with the export of service entry sheets to SAP streamlines the procurement process and enhances efficient across organizations. This integration not only allows for a more organized method of service procurement but also ensures that the data is accurately captured and processed in SAP.

This end-to-end step configuration guide serves as a comprehensive roadmap for organizations looking to implement and optimize complex service procurement with a focus on practical integration with SAP.

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC vs S/4HANA Integration Capabilities: A Practical Guide for Integration Architects
entry.content:
Thank you to everyone who provided technical feedback on the initial version of this guide.

I've updated the document based on your valuable input, specifically:

Added comprehensive coverage of ECC event mechanisms (Change Pointers,
Workflow Events, Output Determination, BAdIs) Clarified BAPI transaction control requirements (BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT)
✅ Enhanced comparison between ECC and S/4HANA event capabilities
✅ Distinguished Event Enablement Framework (S/4 native) from Event Mesh
(BTP cloud service)

This is how the community grows stronger, by sharing knowledge, welcoming corrections, and learning together.

If you spot any areas for improvement or have suggestions based on your real-world experience, please share them. Constructive feedback helps us all build better, more accurate resources for the SAP integration community.

Looking forward to your continued feedback and insights!

@sachin_thigale @Awaddar FYI

entry.title: Re: Table maintenance generator position button question
entry.content:
Hi,
Not knowing your situation in terms of system version and future plans I just wished to give my two cents on this matter.
I would shy away from any form of altering the standard behavior for the table maintenance transactions because this is what builds up that technical debt towards the future.

The options I see are:

  • Use the button as is and just live with it (in essence as @Sandra_Rossi mentioned)
  • Use the filter option from the menu (this is what @striezl mentioned
  • If in an S/4 version of high enough version: 
    • RAP option:
      Stop using SM30 and instead put a CDS on top of the table, bind a behavior definition to it allowing the CRUD operations, expose the cds via service definition, make the service available via service binding (oData V4 UI), and finally generate a list report on the service via BAS or VScode. This gives you an RAP application for the table maintenance where any kind of filtering, sorting, grouping is available to the user who has to maintain this.
      Here is a step by step on this: https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/step-by-step-guide-creating-your-first-rap-application-using-odata-v4-and/ba-p/14308388 
      Or use the RAP generator in eclipse to simplify this process.
    • Extensibility framework - Custom Business Configuration 
      This allows for creation of tables via the extensibility framework in the form of standard Fiori apps.
      The result is the same as with custom RAP, it is just important to note that this option will give you a customizing table (not changeable unless customizing is opened on production or going via transports) while the earlier RAP option allows for transactional tables that can be altered in any system given the right authorizations are in place.
      The fact that this leads to a customizing table (at least in private cloud) is often a reason to take the custom RAP route.
      SAP training on this: https://developers.sap.com/mission.abap-dev-factory-calendar.html  

These days the system we are working on has a high impact on what is available and what is the best option to tackle a business problem.

entry.title: SAP GUI Automation Using VBScript (WScript)
entry.content:
What is WScript and How can it be used to Automate SAP?


WScript is part of the Windows Script Host (WSH), a Microsoft tool for running scripts written in VBScript or JScript. It automates tasks and interacts with the Windows operating system. Scripts can perform actions like file manipulation, registry changes, or automating applications.

SAP provides an option named SAP recording and playback, using that you can record your clicks/actions and run it again as needed. The file is created in .vbs format. See the steps below to do a recording.

Step 1: Click Script recording and playback

Step 2: Save To

Step 3: Call Transaction IW21 and update few fields.

Step 4: Select Save and you will get a notification on Status bar

Step 5: Select Exit and stop recording

Now, you can analyze your VBScript, open with Notepad. Your code will look like as shown below:

If Not IsObject(application) Then
Set SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI")
Set application = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
End If
If Not IsObject(connection) Then
Set connection = application.Children(0)
End If
If Not IsObject(session) Then
Set session = connection.Children(0)
End If
If IsObject(WScript) Then
WScript.ConnectObject session, "on"
WScript.ConnectObject application, "on"
End If

session.findById("wnd[0]").maximize
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "IW21"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtRIWO00-QMART").text = "M2"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtRIWO00-QMART").caretPosition = 2
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subSCREE[shortened command...]").text = "My Notification Short Text"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAB_[shortened command...]").text = "010"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAB_[shortened command...]").text = "0001"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAB_[shortened command...]").text = "MAIN"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAB_[shortened command...]").setFocus
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAB_[shortened command...]").caretPosition = 4
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").press
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[15]").press

Let’s understand the code and syntax

You can use VB script in both UiPath and Power Automate. In UiPath you can use Invoke VB script and in Power Automate desktop you can use Run VB Script.

While automating, it’s very important to get the ‘GuiTextField’ name accurately and then use it in code.

How to get the ‘GuiTextField’ value?

There are two options:

1. SAP GUI Field Help (F1) and Technical Information

While working within the SAP GUI, pressing F1 on a field opens the Performance Assistant, which provides detailed information about that field. To assess technical details:

  • Press F1: While the cursor is in the desired field, press the F1 key.
  • Click on "Technical Information": In the Performance Assistant window, click the "Technical Information" button (usually represented by a hammer and wrench icon).
  • View Field Details: A new window will display the field's technical name, table, data element, domain, and other relevant technical details. Using Selectors from PAD or UiPath, you can find the GuiTextfield value in attributes.

2. Summary of the benefits of VBScript vs. actions for SAP GUI automation

Prerequisites

  • SAP GUI for Windows

  • SAP GUI Scripting enabled on application server

  • SAP GUI Scripting enabled on client

  • Authorization for scripting (parameter: sapgui/user_scripting)

  • Windows Script Host enabled

Best Practices for SAP GUI Scripting

  • Avoid absolute screen coordinates

  • Always reference fields using technical names

  • Handle multiple SAP sessions carefully

  • Avoid hard-coded delays; prefer wait conditions

  • Keep SAP theme consistent across environments

  • Use scripting primarily for stable transactions

Conclusion

SAP GUI scripting remains a powerful option for automating legacy SAP systems where APIs or BAPIs are unavailable. When implemented carefully using technical field identifiers and consistent layouts, VBScript-based automation can provide stable and efficient enterprise automation.

entry.title: My Experience with SAP Generative AI Developer Exam (C_AIG): Excellent Hands-On Format, One Setback
entry.content:
TL;DR: I recently took the SAP Certified – SAP Generative AI Developer (C_AIG) exam in SAP’s new, task-based format. Compared to the traditional proctored, MCQ-style certification (I previously passed the SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud ), this format felt refreshingly real: open-book, hands-on, and aligned with how we actually work: consulting documentation and credible sources to solve practical tasks. The difficulty was fair and thoughtfully designed. However, I faced a serious technical issue in the final minutes: the “Confirm Completion” button did not respond despite multiple attempts, leading to an immediate fail result likely due to the system not registering my submission within the two-hour lab window. I’ve raised a support case and hope SAP can review my completed work or grant a reattempt. Aside from this incident, I genuinely prefer this format over MCQs.



Why This New Format Matters

I appeared this exam after doing the MCQ-based backend ABAP developer certificate. That format is familiar and structured but it inevitably encourages memorization under pressure. The new C_AIG experience is different in the best way:

  • Open-book, real-world problem solving: You are allowed to consult documentation and reputable sources while completing practical tasks. That’s how work in an organization actually happens. The exam felt like a thoughtfully curated, time-boxed lab rather than a memory test.
  • Hands-on skill demonstration: Instead of proving you can recall definitions, you prove you can build and reason, under time constraints, with real tooling.
  • Deeper learning: Solving tasks practically is far more memorable and meaningful than “by-hearting” theory for MCQs. I came away with reinforced concepts and stronger confidence in applying them.

Frankly, I liked this new format very much: it’s unique, modern, and authentic.

Exam Flow: What I Experienced

  • When I started the 24-hour window, I could see the questions and plan my approach.
  • Once the system access was granted, the two-hour practical lab timer started.
  • The tasks were well-thought-out, reasonably tough, and lengthy in a productive way. You need both breadth and depth to complete them in time but they are absolutely solvable with focus and good time management.
  • Importantly, nothing exceeded the learning journey it was rigorous but fair. No trickery, just practical application of what you’re supposed to know.

This balance of open-book realism and tight timeboxing made it a genuinely strong assessment model.

 

The Technical Issue That Cost Me the Attempt

With ~10 minutes left on my two-hour lab window, I had completed all tasks, verified my answers, and proceeded, as instructed: to click “Confirm Completion” (the button near the “Get Access” control).

  • I clicked repeatedly.
  • I closed and reopened the browser.
  • I reopened the certification link.
  • I even re-established system access to the Generative AI environment.

Despite all that, there was no response from the “Confirm Completion” button. As the clock ran out, the portal eventually returned an instant failure. Based on the message and timing, it most likely meant the system did not register my submission within the two-hour lab period, even though I had completed and verified my work within time.

What makes it upsetting is that my work was done. I did what the assessment asked, thoroughly and on time, but the submission mechanism failed me. I’ve since registered a customer support case detailing the problem. I sincerely hope SAP can either:

  1. Verify my completed tasks from their system logs or workspace and give provide me with the honest result or certification, or
  2. Grant a fresh attempt, given the submission failure was technical in nature.

I’m a student who had 1 limited attempt for this certification which I really wanted to get, and losing one to a glitch, after honest preparation and timely completion was frustrating.

Closing Thoughts

The C_AIG exam’s design deserves recognition: it’s modern, fair, and genuinely skill-focused. My experience was positive until the submission glitch which has gotten me real frustrated and I hope for a quicker response from the support. I’m sharing this not to discourage, but to advocate for a smoother, more resilient submission process that matches the quality of the exam itself. I hope my case helps improve the candidate experience for everyone who follows.
